data.title: U.S., Australia back rare earths refinery with up to $600M in potential funding
data.description:
Australia and the United States have signaled support for a major rare earths processing project, offering potential financing of roughly A$849 million (about $600 million) for a refinery being developed by Tronox Holdings (TROX).

According to a joint statement on Sunday, Export Finance Australia and the U.S. Export-Import Bank each issued preliminary letters indicating they could provide up to A$424 million toward the project. The planned refinery would operate across sites in Western Australia and the United States.

The facility is expected to leverage existing mining and processing infrastructure to produce mixed rare earth carbonate, including both light and heavy elements. These materials are essential for applications ranging from defense systems to advanced manufacturing and clean energy technologies.

In a parallel move, the two agencies also expressed potential support of up to A$500 million each for the Kalgoorlie Nickel Project being advanced by Ardea Resources Ltd. in Western Australia. The development is described as holding the country’s largest known nickel-cobalt resource.

The financing signals come as Washington and Canberra deepen cooperation to secure access to critical minerals, building on a partnership framework agreed at the White House last year.

data.title: Trump orders immediate blockade of Strait of Hormuz after talks collapse
data.description:
Donald Trump said the United States would move to block maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz after negotiations with Iran unraveled over the weekend in Islamabad.

In a social media statement, Trump said the U.S. Navy would immediately begin efforts to stop vessels from entering or leaving the critical shipping lane, calling it a necessary response to escalating tensions.

 

 

The announcement followed the abrupt end of marathon talks involving JD Vance, along with envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, who spent nearly a full day in negotiations with senior Iranian officials. The discussions, brokered by Pakistan, were aimed at halting a six-week conflict but failed to produce an agreement, leaving a fragile ceasefire in doubt.

Iranian officials signaled that U.S. demands had been too steep, while the country’s foreign ministry suggested that further talks could still take place, noting that major disputes are unlikely to be resolved in a single round.

Trump also accused Iran of threatening global commerce by considering measures such as mining the strait or charging ships for safe passage. He characterized such actions as coercive and said the U.S. would not tolerate them.

Expanding on the policy, Trump warned that American forces would also target ships that comply with any Iranian-imposed transit fees, stating that vessels paying such charges should not expect protection in international waters.

The Strait of Hormuz is a vital artery for global energy supplies, handling roughly 20% of the world’s oil and liquefied natural gas shipments, and any disruption there could have far-reaching economic consequences.

Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Wall Street trading desks poised for $40B quarter amid geopolitical turmoil
data.description:
Major U.S. banks are on track to post more than $40 billion in trading revenue for the first quarter, as heightened geopolitical tensions helped drive sharp movements across financial markets, the Financial Times reported Sunday.

Institutions including JPMorgan Chase (JPM), Goldman Sachs (GS), Morgan Stanley (MS), Citigroup (C) and Bank of America (BAC) are expected to report their strongest combined quarterly trading performance in more than a decade, based on analyst estimates.

Forecasts suggest the group’s trading income will rise roughly 13% compared with the same period last year, even against a backdrop of already elevated volatility in early 2025 driven by policies under Donald Trump.

Middle East, Venezuela hot spots

Market turbulence intensified further in the latest quarter, fueled by conflict in the Middle East and U.S. military actions tied to Venezuela, which triggered large swings in commodities, equities and currencies. Analysts say such conditions tend to boost trading activity, as clients reposition portfolios and hedge risks.

The unrest contributed to a surge in oil prices alongside declines in equities, raising concerns that higher energy costs could stoke inflation and weigh on global growth. Recent data shows the conflict has indeed driven energy-led price pressures and market uncertainty.

Within trading divisions, equities are expected to deliver the strongest gains, outpacing fixed income, currencies and commodities. Analysts project equity trading revenue growth in the mid-teens percentage range, compared with high-single to low-double-digit increases for FICC businesses.

At the same time, banks are benefiting from structural shifts made after the 2008 financial crisis. Trading desks today are less focused on taking large directional bets and more oriented toward facilitating client activity and providing liquidity, which allows them to capitalize on periods of elevated volatility.

Investment banking divisions are also likely to show improvement. Fee income from advisory and underwriting is projected to rise by more than 10% across the major banks, supported by a rebound in dealmaking. Increased financing tied to artificial intelligence projects and a more accommodating regulatory environment have helped revive activity after a prolonged slowdown.

Volatility’s consequences

Even so, the outlook is not without risks. Analysts warn that sustained geopolitical instability could dampen capital markets activity, particularly in equities, where volatile conditions may discourage companies from pursuing initial public offerings or other listings, the FT reported.

Overall, profits at the largest banks are expected to increase by around 7%, with firms more heavily exposed to trading and investment banking, such as Goldman Sachs (GS) and Morgan Stanley (MS), likely to see the biggest gains.

Earnings season is set to begin with Goldman Sachs (GS), followed by J.P. Morgan (JPM) and Citigroup (C), with Morgan Stanley (MS) and Bank of America (BAC) reporting shortly thereafter.

Investors will also be watching banks’ exposure to private credit markets, where recent fund outflows have raised concerns about asset quality. Lending to hedge funds and private capital firms has expanded significantly in recent years, offering higher returns but also introducing new risks in a more uncertain environment.

data.title: Quant snapshot: JinkoSolar, PNC Financial lead top-rated names as SL Green Realty, Badger Meter lag
data.description:
Quant rankings for the upcoming earnings calendar show strength concentrated in financials and select technology and consumer staples names, while weakness is more pronounced across real estate, technology, and pockets of consumer discretionary and energy.

This week’s top-rated names are led by JinkoSolar Holding (JKS), which holds a quant rating of 4.78, followed by a strong showing from financials including PNC Financial Services (PNC) at 4.67, First Horizon (FHN) at 4.63, and Citizens Financial Group (CFG) at 4.57. Consumer staples name PepsiCo (PEP), also at 4.57, features prominently alongside technology names such as Ericsson (ERIC). Additional financial heavyweights, including State Street (STT), Bank of America (BAC), and Truist Financial (TFC), round out the higher-ranked cohort, alongside industrial player Insteel Industries (IIIN), reflecting broadly solid factor readings across valuation, growth, and profitability.

On the downside, real estate and select technology names dominate the weakest-ranked stocks. SL Green Realty (SLG) holds the lowest quant score at 1.55, followed closely by Badger Meter (BMI) at 1.59 and Lakeland Industries (LAKE) at 1.72. Other laggards include Great Southern Bancorp (GSBC) and MIND Technology (MIND), pointing to weaker factor trends across valuation and momentum. Additional names such as SurgePays (SURG), New Horizon Aircraft (HOVR), and Wipro (WIT) also feature among the lower-ranked stocks, alongside Autoliv (ALV) and Barry Callebaut (BYCBF), reflecting mixed but generally softer quantitative profiles.

Other high-profile companies set to report earnings this week include Netflix (NFLX), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM), and JPMorgan Chase (JPM), all of which carry Hold ratings with quant scores of 3.22, 3.44, 3.49, and 3.33, respectively.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system grades stocks based on their relative performance on critical quantitative measures, including valuation, growth, stock momentum, and profitability. Ratings are assigned on a scale from 1 to 5, with any score of 3.5 or above considered a bullish rating and any score of 2.5 or below indicating a bearish assessment.

Here are the top-rated upcoming earnings stocks, ranked by Quant:

  • JinkoSolar Holding (JKS), Quant Rating: 4.78.
  • PNC Financial Services (PNC), Quant Rating: 4.67.
  • First Horizon (FHN), Quant Rating: 4.63.
  • Citizens Financial Group (CFG), Quant Rating: 4.57.
  • PepsiCo (PEP), Quant Rating: 4.57.
  • Ericsson (ERIC), Quant Rating: 4.55.
  • State Street (STT), Quant Rating: 4.45.
  • Bank of America (BAC), Quant Rating: 4.38.
  • Insteel Industries (IIIN), Quant Rating: 4.30.
  • Truist Financial (TFC), Quant Rating: 4.20.

Here are the bottom-rated upcoming earnings stocks, ranked by Quant:

  • SL Green Realty (SLG), Quant Rating: 1.55.
  • Badger Meter (BMI), Quant Rating: 1.59.
  • Lakeland Industries (LAKE), Quant Rating: 1.72.
  • Great Southern Bancorp (GSBC), Quant Rating: 2.30.
  • MIND Technology (MIND), Quant Rating: 2.30.
  • SurgePays (SURG), Quant Rating: 2.50.
  • New Horizon Aircraft (HOVR), Quant Rating: 2.54.
  • Wipro (WIT), Quant Rating: 2.64.
  • Autoliv (ALV), Quant Rating: 2.72.
  • Barry Callebaut (BYCBF), Quant Rating: 2.78.

data.title: Iran targets rapid energy recovery after strikes, aims for partial capacity in weeks
data.description:
Iran is working to bring much of its damaged refining and fuel distribution network back online, with officials targeting a return to roughly 70% to 80% of pre-strike capacity within one to two months, according to a senior oil official.

Mohammad Sadeq Azimifar said in remarks to the Student News Network that repair efforts are already underway following recent attacks on key energy infrastructure.

He added that sections of the Lavan refinery could restart operations in as little as 10 days, with additional units expected to come back in stages as restoration work progresses.

data.title: Notable healthcare headlines for the week: Organon, Eli Lilly, and Gilead in focus
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages struggled on Friday, with both the S&P 500 and the Dow in the red, as the core retail inflation print for March came in slightly cooler than expected, while investors continued to brace for U.S.-Iran war tensions.

The benchmark S&P 500 closed -0.1%, while the heavy-tech Nasdaq Composite ended +0.4%, and the blue-chip Dow finished -0.6%.

Week-to-date, however, all indexes took home gains. The S&P 500 was +3.6%, the Nasdaq was +4.7%, and the Dow was +5.5%.

The S&P 500 Health Care Index Sector (XLV)  also gained about 0.7% during the week.

The top S&P 500 healthcare gainers and losers for the last week are as follows:

Top Gainers:

UnitedHealth Group (UNH) +9.76%

Humana (HUM) +8.05%

CVS Health (CVS) +7.95%

Centene (CNC) +6.24%

Waters Corporation (WAT) +5.18%

Top Losers:

Pfizer (PFE) -4.94%

Labcorp (LH) -4.67%

Edwards Lifesciences (EW) -3.92%

Insulet Corporation (PODD) -3.61%

IQVIA (IQV) -3.42%

Here are some of the important healthcare stories from this week:

Managed care rebounds after Medicare rate decision

Managed care stocks bounced back from a year-to-date decline to reach a three-month high on Tuesday after the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) finalized a better-than-expected reimbursement rate for Medicare Advantage plans for 2027.

Health insurers with sizable operations in the MA market, such as UnitedHealth (UNH), Humana (HUM), and Alignment Healthcare (ALHC), have led the gainers. Others, including CVS Health (CVS), Molina Healthcare (MOH), Elevance Health (ELV), Oscar Health (OSCR), Clover Health (CLOV), Centene (CNC), and Cigna (CI), are also in the green.

While the proposed average payment hike for Medicare Advantage plans stood at 0.09% earlier this year, the final rate, issued on Monday after the market close, indicated a 2.48% increase, triggering bullish views on the sector.

Amazon to sell Eli Lilly’s weight loss pill via same-day delivery

Amazon (AMZN) Pharmacy is now offering Eli Lilly’s (LLY) recently approved weight loss pill, Foundayo, across nearly 3,000 cities and towns in the U.S. via its same-day delivery service, the company announced on Thursday.

Cash-paying customers with a valid prescription can access the once-daily pill at $5 per day or $149 per month, while those with insurance can buy it at $1 per day or $25 per month, the e-commerce giant said.

Soon after a medical appointment, patients can also purchase the GLP-1 therapy at in-office kiosks available at some of its One Medical locations.

The company plans to expand its same-day delivery service to nearly 4,500 U.S. localities by the end of this year.

On the other hand, Novo Nordisk (NVO) (NONOF) announced on Tuesday that its newly approved 7.2 mg version of its blockbuster obesity therapy Wegovy is now available in the U.S., allowing patients to benefit from the highest dose of the popular GLP-1 medicine.

Branded as Wegovy HD, the injection will cost $399 per month for cash-paying customers and as little as $25 per month for those with commercial insurance, the Danish drugmaker said. Novo (NVO) sells the lowest 0.25 mg dose of Wegovy at $199 per month for self-paying customers.

Organon rises as Sun Pharma is said to near $12B buyout deal

Organon (OGN) shares jumped ~28% on Friday after media reports from India indicated that Mumbai-based generic drugmaker Sun Pharmaceutical Industries had submitted a $12B binding offer for the U.S. company.

According to the reports, Sun Pharma has reached the final stages of the bid after completing over three months of due diligence. The company is finalizing financing with the support of global banking giants, including JPMorgan (JPM) and MUFG, the reports added.

While Sun Pharmaceutical called the latest report on the deal from India's The Economic Times newspaper speculative in nature, BNP Paribas Equity Research argued that a deal with Sun could be positive for Organon (OGN).

A potential acquisition would make the transaction the largest overseas M&A deal by an Indian pharmaceutical company. Sun Pharmaceutical shares fell 3% in Mumbai in reaction to the reports.

Gilead to acquire Tubulis GmbH for $5B

Gilead (GILD) said on Tuesday that it would acquire Germany-based biotechnology firm Tubulis GmbH for $5B to build on its oncology pipeline.

The acquisition will give Gilead access to key assets from Tubulis, including antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) therapies for ovarian cancer and other solid tumors.

Under the terms of the transaction, Gilead will acquire all the outstanding equity of Tubulis for an upfront cash payment of $3.15B, payable at closing, and up to $1.85B in contingent milestone payments. The transaction is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026.

Following the close of the transaction, Tubulis will operate as a dedicated ADC research organization within Gilead, with the Munich site serving as a hub for ADC innovation, manufacturing, and clinical capabilities, the company added.

TrumpRx adds medicines from AbbVie and Roche’s Genentech: report

AbbVie (ABBV) and Roche’s (RHHBY) Genentech unit have become the latest drugmakers to offer their medicines on TrumpRx, a government-run direct-to-consumer platform launched earlier this year, according to CBS News.

The moves will make AbbVie (ABBV) and Roche (RHHBF) the 10th and 11th drugmakers selling medicines on the site, which provides some popular medications to cash-paying customers at sharply discounted prices.

AbbVie’s (ABBV) blockbuster arthritis therapy Humira will be available on TrumpRx at an 86% discount as part of a drug pricing deal the Trump administration reached with the U.S. drugmaker in January.

According to another pricing deal with the White House in December, Genentech (RHHBY) agreed to sell its flu medicine Xofluza for $50 for direct purchases, down from a list price of roughly $168.

data.title: Vitol trading bet backfires as Iran conflict jolts oil markets: WSJ
data.description:
A high-profile trading team at Vitol suffered losses totaling several hundred million dollars at the outset of the Iran conflict after a series of energy market wagers moved in the wrong direction, The Wall Street Journal reported Saturday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The team is led by Yaoyao Liu, a well-known figure in commodities trading circles, recognized for placing large and often profitable derivatives trades. Industry observers had long speculated about the scale and nature of his positions. Among the trades believed to have gone against him were bets that diesel would outperform jet fuel and that Dubai crude would weaken relative to Brent.

Those positions were effectively predicated on a de-escalation scenario, where geopolitical tensions would ease. Instead, the outbreak of war and Iran’s move to close the Strait of Hormuz sent energy prices sharply in the opposite direction, undermining the strategy.

Despite the setback, some of the losses have reportedly been recouped, and the firm overall remains profitable for the year, according to sources.

Major trading volumes

Vitol occupies a central role in global energy markets, acting as a key intermediary between producers and consumers while managing the flow of millions of barrels of oil and other commodities each day. Founded in Rotterdam in the 1960s, the firm now trades roughly 8 million barrels of oil daily and maintains investments spanning storage, refining, power generation and retail fuel networks across regions including the United States and West Africa.

The company’s scale and reach have also made it a strategic partner in geopolitics. Following the reported capture of Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro earlier this year, the Trump administration reportedly turned to Vitol and rival Trafigura to help revive the country’s oil exports.

In 2025, Vitol generated approximately $343 billion in revenue, surpassing Exxon Mobil (XOM), and remains privately owned by roughly 600 employees.

Hedge fund similarities

Liu, who joined Vitol in 2012 after working at Goldman Sachs, runs a global trading operation spanning Dubai, London and Houston. His group functions in many ways like an internal hedge fund, focusing on financial contracts linked to oil and fuel markets rather than the firm’s physical trading operations.

The volatility triggered by the conflict created broader operational challenges for Vitol beyond trading losses. Within weeks of the escalation involving U.S. and Israeli strikes, vessels carrying the firm’s fuel were attacked in the Persian Gulf, resulting in a fatality. The company also secured a $3 billion credit facility as a precaution against margin calls driven by extreme price swings, though it has not drawn on it.

Logistical disruptions compounded the pressure. Cargoes became stranded due to the Hormuz blockade, forcing the company to scramble for alternative supplies to meet customer obligations. At the same time, hedging positions tied to Middle Eastern shipments effectively turned into bets against a rapidly rising crude market.

While other trading firms faced similar disruptions, Vitol’s exposure was particularly significant given its longstanding presence in the Persian Gulf and its reliance on regional supply flows. By contrast, the trading arm of TotalEnergies (TTE) (TTE:CA) (TTEF) reportedly benefited from correctly anticipating a surge in Dubai crude prices.

Even so, Vitol’s financial position remains relatively strong. The firm has built substantial reserves in recent years, benefiting from market turbulence during the Covid-19 pandemic and the war in Ukraine. Its lower reliance on debt compared with some competitors also provides flexibility in navigating ongoing volatility.

The episode marks a rare stumble for a company that has historically prided itself on avoiding major missteps in turbulent markets, underscoring how quickly geopolitical shocks can upend even the most sophisticated trading strategies, the Journal reported.

data.title: Earnings week ahead: JNJ, NFLX, TSM, GS, C, JPM, BAC, PEP, and more
data.description:
Earnings season kicks off next week, with a wave of reports from major banks, tech leaders, and industrial names set to provide an early read on the economic and corporate landscape.

Financials will take center stage, with results from JPMorgan Chase (JPM), Bank of America (BAC), Citigroup (C), Wells Fargo & Company (WFC), The Goldman Sachs Group (GS), and Morgan Stanley (MS), alongside asset manager BlackRock (BLK).

Regional lenders, including U.S. Bancorp (USB), Truist Financial (TFC), The PNC Financial Services Group (PNC), The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation (BK), KeyCorp (KEY), Ally Financial (ALLY), and M&T Bank (MTB), will further shape expectations around lending, deposits, and credit quality.

In technology and global demand, Netflix (NFLX) and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (TSM) will be closely watched for signals on streaming growth and semiconductor demand tied to AI and electronics.

Consumer and healthcare names such as PepsiCo (PEP), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), and Abbott Laboratories (ABT) will offer insight into pricing power, demand resilience, and healthcare trends.

Meanwhile, industrial and cyclical players, including Alcoa Corporation (AA), Fastenal Company (FAST), Prologis (PLD), The Travelers Companies (TRV), and Telefonaktiebolaget LM Ericsson (publ) (ERIC), will provide a broader read on global industrial activity, insurance trends, and infrastructure spending.

With a strong mix of financials, tech, and cyclicals reporting, the first wave of earnings could set the tone for markets in the weeks ahead.

Below is a rundown of major quarterly updates anticipated in the week of April 13 to April 17:

Monday, April 13

Goldman Sachs Group (GS)

The Goldman Sachs Group (GS) is set to report Q1 results before Monday’s open, with analysts expecting ~16% Y/Y EPS growth and ~12% revenue expansion.

CEO David Solomon recently highlighted a more favorable regulatory backdrop, which could drive a pickup in dealmaking and support returns above the bank’s mid-teens targets. Growth in asset and wealth management, where the firm is targeting 17%–19% returns over the next few years, remains a key focus, though management cautions that the path may be uneven amid geopolitical risks and AI-driven market volatility.

Wall Street maintains a Buy rating, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant system is more cautious at Hold, citing valuation and profitability concerns.

SA contributor Jeremy LaKosh remains bullish, pointing to recent share price weakness as an attractive entry point. Strength in global banking, particularly investment banking and equities trading, continues to support earnings, while asset management provides stability. Ongoing cost initiatives, including layoffs and the “One Goldman Sachs 3.0” strategy, aim to enhance efficiency through automation and AI.

Key risks include interest rate uncertainty and potential stress in private credit markets, though GS’s diversified model and strategic initiatives support a bullish outlook.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $16.35
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $16.89B
  • Earnings Insight: Goldman Sachs has beaten EPS expectations for eight consecutive quarters and revenue expectations in 7 of those reports.

Also reporting: Fastenal (FAST), Sify Technologies (SIFY), and more.

Tuesday, April 14

JPMorgan Chase (JPM)

JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) is set to report Q1 results before Tuesday’s open alongside Wells Fargo & Company (WFC) and Citigroup Inc. (C), with analysts expecting ~8% growth in both revenue and earnings.

CEO Jamie Dimon recently downplayed systemic risks from the rapid rise of private credit, noting the ~$1.8T market remains small relative to the broader financial system. However, he flagged emerging vulnerabilities, including weaker underwriting standards, increased use of payment-in-kind structures, and limited transparency, which could amplify losses in a downturn. Higher rates and wider spreads may further strain borrowers, while losses in leveraged lending could exceed expectations as the cycle turns.

Dimon also raised concerns about private equity, highlighting extended holding periods and questioning how these assets would perform in a prolonged downturn.

Wall Street maintains a Buy rating on JPM, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant system is more cautious at Hold, citing valuation and profitability concerns.

SA contributor Jeremy LaKosh remains bullish ahead of Q1, supported by macro resilience and sector strength. Expectations include modest balance sheet growth, with lending up ~2% and deposits rising ~1%. Net interest income is likely to improve on stable spreads, helping offset investment banking headwinds. Despite risks, improving earnings expectations and relatively attractive valuation continue to support a constructive outlook.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $5.49
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $48.91B
  • Earnings Insight: The bank has exceeded EPS and revenue expectations in 7 of the past 8 quarters.

Also reporting: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), Citigroup (C), Wells Fargo (WFC), BlackRock (BLK), CarMax (KMX), Albertsons (ACI), and more.

Wednesday, April 15

Bank of America (BAC)

Bank of America Corporation (BAC) is set to report Q1 results before Wednesday’s open, with analysts expecting EPS to rise over 12% on ~9% Y/Y revenue growth.

Wall Street maintains a Strong Buy rating, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant system was recently upgraded to Buy from Hold.

In contrast, SA contributor Vladimir Dimitrov remains cautious with a Hold, citing growing macro and sector pressures. BAC’s 5–7% net interest income growth outlook for 2026 faces risks from a flatter yield curve and reduced expectations for rate cuts. Commercial loan growth, a key driver in 2025, slowed in Q4, while consumer lending remains fragile amid weakening confidence. He believes that Q1 results will be key in assessing BAC’s resilience, with potential downside risks to profitability and loan growth.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $1.01
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $29.95B
  • Earnings Insight: The bank has exceeded EPS and revenue expectations in 7 of the past 8 quarters.

Also reporting: Morgan Stanley (MS), ASML Holding (ASML), M&T Bank (MTB), PNC Financial Services Group (PNC), SL Green Realty (SLG), Progressive (PGR), J.B. Hunt Transport Services (JBHT), First Horizon National Corp (FHN), and more.

Thursday, April 16

Netflix (NFLX)

Streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) is set to report Q1 results after Tuesday’s close, with analysts expecting over 15% Y/Y growth in both revenue and earnings.

With major strategic distractions behind it, Netflix is refocusing on content investment, including live events, and shareholder returns. Goldman Sachs Group recently upgraded the stock to Buy, with analyst Eric Sheridan highlighting a multi-year growth runway driven by advertising (projected to reach ~$9.5B by 2030) and pricing power.

Wall Street maintains a Buy rating, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant system remains at Hold on valuation concerns.

SA contributor Vinay Utham downgraded Netflix to Hold, citing fair valuation and limited margin for error heading into Q1. He sees ad growth and live sports expansion as key catalysts, focusing on management's ~$3B FY2026 ad revenue target. Valuation suggests a balanced risk-reward, with a base case near current levels and a more attractive entry on a pullback or improved ad guidance.

In contrast, Investing Group Leader Amrita Roy maintains a Buy, highlighting strong fundamentals and upside potential. The FY2026 outlook includes 12–14% revenue growth, ad revenue doubling to ~$3B, and margins expanding to ~31.5%. Key triggers include Q1 beats, progress on advertising, limited churn post price hikes, and potential upward guidance revisions.

While near-term valuation appears balanced, longer-term growth in advertising and earnings continues to support a constructive outlook.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $0.77
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $12.17B
  • Earnings Insight: Netflix has risen above EPS and revenue estimates in 7 of the past 8 quarters.

Also reporting: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (TSM), PepsiCo (PEP), Abbott (ABT), Alcoa (AA), U.S. Bancorp (USB), Prologis (PLD), Charles Schwab (SCHW), JinkoSolar Holding (JKS), Bank of New York Mellon (BK), and more.

Friday, April 17

State Street (STT)

State Street Corporation (STT) is set to report Q1 results before Friday’s open, with analysts expecting earnings to rise over 28% Y/Y on ~12% revenue growth.

Wall Street maintains a Buy rating, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant system recently downgraded it to Buy from Strong Buy.

SA author Albert Anthony reiterates a Buy on State Street Corporation (STT), citing meaningful upside driven by organic growth, resilient markets, and an improving risk profile, along with a well-covered dividend supported by a consistent track record across cycles. He also highlights the bank’s strong credit profile, with A-level ratings from Fitch Ratings, Moody's Investors Service, and S&P Global Ratings, and its designation as a systemically important institution by the Financial Stability Board.

While positioning STT favorably against peers such as The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation ((BK)), Invesco Ltd. ((IVZ)) and Northern Trust Corporation ((NTRS)), he notes that interest rate risk remains a factor given the company’s loan exposure alongside its core custody and asset-servicing businesses.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $2.62
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $3.69B
  • Earnings Insight: STT has beaten revenue expectations in 7 of the past 8 quarters and EPS estimates in all the reports.
Also reporting: Ericsson (ERIC), Truist Financial (TFC), Regions Financial (RF), Ally Financial (ALLY), Fifth Third Bancorp (FITB), and more.

data.title: Trump hints at use of naval blockade of Hormuz to pressure Iran
data.description:
President Donald Trump indicated in an online post early Sunday he may use a naval blockade if Iran refuses to accept peace terms from the U.S.

Trump reposted an article titled "The Trump card the president holds if Iran won’t bend: a naval blockade" from online outlet Just the News.

According to the report, military experts say the U.S. Navy could effectively control shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, potentially choking off Iran’s oil exports and increasing economic pressure on Tehran.

The strategy would rely on a growing U.S. naval presence in the region, including major carrier groups capable of monitoring and restricting maritime traffic near key export points like Kharg Island.

The blockade concept has gained attention after U.S.-Iran talks in Pakistan ended without agreement, with Washington presenting what officials described as a “final” proposal.

Analysts say such a move could significantly disrupt global oil flows and increase pressure not only on Iran but also on major buyers such as China and India, raising the stakes for energy markets and geopolitics.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: S&P down, oil up on blockchain after U.S.-Iran talks end with no deal
data.description:
Markets saw a predictable knee-jerk reaction Sunday after peace talks between the U.S. and Iran in Pakistan ended without an agreement.

Vice President J.D. Vance said the U.S. needs “an affirmative commitment” that Iran will not pursue a nuclear weapon or the capabilities to quickly develop one.

Early trading signals pointed to a risk-off move. On the Hyperliquid blockchain, which trades 24/7, S&P 500-linked contracts (SPY) (IVV) (VOO) fell about 50 points, while oil prices (USO) (BNO) moved higher.

Weekend trading via contracts for difference on IG showed U.S. crude initially jumping to $98/bbl from $90/bbl before coming off highs to around $96.

Meanwhile, bitcoin (BTC-USD) weakened, falling roughly 2%.

The moves highlight continued sensitivity across asset classes to geopolitical developments, particularly around the Strait of Hormuz and broader Middle East tensions.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.
